# HireVue

HireVue launched its voice-based AI Interviewer in mid-June 2026, running two-way conversational screens 24/7 and scoring candidates against IO-validated rubrics. It is HireVue's first product since the March 2026 Hireguide acquisition, and it pushes automated evaluation further up the funnel — the candidate sees a conversational agent, not a form. For TA leaders considering AI-conducted interviews, HireVue's design raises hard questions: candidate experience, audit trails, fairness across accents and speech patterns, and what "IO-validated" means when the assessment logic itself is proprietary and evolving.

## Timeline

- **18 Jun 2026** — HireVue launched its voice-based AI Interviewer, running two-way conversational screens 24/7 and scoring candidates against IO-validated rubrics — its first product since the March Hireguide acquisition. It pushes automated evaluation earlier in the funnel, so weigh candidate experience and audit trails before deploying at scale. — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/hirevue-ai-interviewer-sets-new-enterprise-standard-grounded-in-validated-hiring-science-302803800.html)

## Open questions

- How does HireVue's voice-based agent handle accents, speech patterns, or disabilities that affect audio quality, and how is that variance audited?
- What does "IO-validated" mean when the agent itself updates in production?
